# Get 1Password pricing details - look for price numbers
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find dollar amounts
prices = re.findall(r'\$\d+\.?\d*', content)
print('Prices found:', prices[:30])
# Find plan names
plans = re.findall(r'[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s[A-Z][a-z]+)*', content)
print('Plan-like strings:', [p for p in plans if 'business' in p.lower() or 'team' in p.lower()][:20])
"

# Let me try to use Python to parse the 1Password page for pricing data
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for pricing data in JSON-like structures
# Search for plan names and prices
plan_sections = re.findall(r'"(Business|Enterprise|Teams|Team|Starter|Professional|Individual)"[^"]*"[^"]*"\$?(\d+\.?\d*)', content)
print("Plan-price pairs:", plan_sections[:20])

# Look for price per user patterns
per_user = re.findall(r'[\$]?(\d+\.?\d{2})\s*/\s*(?:user|seat|month|year)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Per-user prices:", per_user[:20])

# Look for any dollar amounts near "business"
business_context = re.findall(r'.{0,50}business.{0,50}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for ctx in business_context[:5]:
    prices = re.findall(r'\$\d+\.?\d*', ctx)
    if prices:
        print(f"Business context with price: {ctx[:200]}")
EOF

# Get more context around those prices from 1Password
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find context around each price
prices = ['2.99', '2.65', '3.75', '4.99', '4.75', '5.99']
for p in prices:
    idx = content.find(p)
    if idx >= 0:
        context = content[max(0,idx-200):idx+200]
        # Clean up for readability
        context = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', context)
        print(f"Price ${p}:")
        print(f"  Context: ...{context}...")
        print()
EOF

# Parse Bitwarden pricing page
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
with open('/tmp/bitwarden_pricing3.html') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find all dollar amounts
prices = re.findall(r'[\$]?(\d+\.?\d{2})', content)
unique_prices = sorted(set(prices))
print("Unique prices found:", unique_prices[:30])

# Find context around prices
for p in unique_prices[:15]:
    idx = content.find(p)
    if idx >= 0:
        context = content[max(0,idx-150):idx+150]
        context = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', context)
        print(f"\nPrice ${p}:")
        print(f"  ...{context[:300]}...")
EOF

# Let me try to get 1Password business pricing from their business page
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
with open('/tmp/1password_business_pricing.html') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for business plan pricing
# Search for plan names near prices
patterns = [
    r'"(Business|Enterprise|Teams)"[^"]{0,200}?\$?(\d+\.?\d*)',
    r'\$?(\d+\.?\d{2})\s*(?:USD|per\s+user|per\s+month|per\s+seat)',
    r'"price"[^"]*"\$?(\d+\.?\d*)"',
]
for p in patterns:
    matches = re.findall(p, content, re.IGNORECASE)
    if matches:
        print(f"Pattern {p}: {matches[:15]}")

# Also look for "Business" plan context
business_matches = re.findall(r'.{0,100}Business.{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for m in business_matches[:10]:
    prices = re.findall(r'\$\d+\.?\d*', m)
    if prices:
        print(f"Business context: {m[:200]}")
EOF

# Parse Bitwarden pricing properly
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
with open('/tmp/bitwarden_pricing3.html') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for pricing data in structured format
# Search for plan names and prices
plan_data = re.findall(r'"(Team|Teams|Enterprise|Business|Individual|Free)"[^"]{0,300}?"price"[^"]*"?(\$?\d+\.?\d*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Plan-price pairs:", plan_data[:20])

# Look for per user pricing
per_user = re.findall(r'(\$?\d+\.?\d{2})\s*/\s*(?:user|seat|month|year)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Per-user prices:", per_user[:20])

# Look for pricing sections
pricing_sections = re.findall(r'(Team|Enterprise|Business)[^<]{0,200}?\$?(\d+\.?\d*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Plan sections:", pricing_sections[:20])

# Try to find the actual pricing table data
# Look for JSON-like pricing data
json_data = re.findall(r'price[^"]*":\s*"?\$?(\d+\.?\d*)', content)
print("JSON price values:", sorted(set(json_data))[:30])
EOF

# Let me try to use a Python script to fetch pricing from multiple sources
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import json
import re

# Try to get Bitwarden pricing from their pricing page with different user agents
url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36',
    'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.5',
})
try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
        content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
        print(f"Bitwarden pricing page size: {len(content)}")
        # Look for pricing data
        prices = re.findall(r'\$\d+\.?\d{2}', content)
        print(f"Prices found: {sorted(set(prices))[:20]}")
        # Look for Team plan pricing
        team = re.findall(r'Team.{0,200}?\$\d+\.?\d{2}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        print(f"Team pricing contexts: {team[:5]}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
PYEOF

# Get more Bitwarden pricing context - look for all prices
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import re

url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
    content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Find all dollar amounts with context
pattern = r'(\$?\d+\.?\d{2})'
for match in re.finditer(pattern, content):
    price = match.group(1)
    if float(price.replace('$','')) > 1:  # Only meaningful prices
        idx = match.start()
        context = content[max(0,idx-200):idx+200]
        context = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', context)
        context = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', context).strip()
        # Only show if near plan names
        if any(w in context.lower() for w in ['team', 'enterprise', 'business', 'individual', 'free', 'per']):
            print(f"Price {price}:")
            print(f"  ...{context[:300]}...")
            print()
PYEOF

# Let me get the full pricing data from the Bitwarden main pricing page more carefully
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import re
import json

url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
    content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# The pricing data is in JSON format embedded in the page
# Look for the pricing JSON data
# Search for "Team" plan pricing
team_section = re.search(r'"Team".{0,500}?"price".{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
if team_section:
    print("Team section:", team_section.group()[:500])

# Search for "Enterprise" plan pricing
ent_section = re.search(r'"Enterprise".{0,500}?"price".{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
if ent_section:
    print("Enterprise section:", ent_section.group()[:500])

# Search for all pricing entries
pricing_entries = re.findall(r'"internalName"[^}]+}', content)
for entry in pricing_entries[:30]:
    if 'price' in entry.lower() or 'pricing' in entry.lower():
        print(entry[:300])
        print("---")
PYEOF

# Let me try to extract pricing from Bitwarden's page by looking at the JSON structure
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import re
import json

url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
    content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Look for pricing data in the JSON structure
# Search for "Team" plan
team_matches = re.findall(r'"Team"[^"]*"[^"]*"\$?(\d+\.?\d*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Team price matches:", team_matches[:10])

# Search for pricing in a different way
# Look for "per user" or "per month" near prices
per_user = re.findall(r'(\$?\d+\.?\d{2})\s*(?:/|per)\s*(?:user|month|seat)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Per user/month prices:", per_user[:10])

# Look for the pricing table data
# Search for "Team" plan with price
team_price = re.findall(r'Team.{0,300}?\$\d+\.?\d{2}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
print("Team pricing contexts:", len(team_price))
for t in team_price[:3]:
    t_clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', t)
    t_clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', t_clean).strip()
    print(f"  ...{t_clean[:300]}...")

# Search for "Enterprise" plan with price
ent_price = re.findall(r'Enterprise.{0,300}?\$\d+\.?\d{2}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
print("Enterprise pricing contexts:", len(ent_price))
for e in ent_price[:3]:
    e_clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', e)
    e_clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', e_clean).strip()
    print(f"  ...{e_clean[:300]}...")
PYEOF

import urllib.request
import re

url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36',
})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
    content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Look for pricing data in the JSON structure
# Search for "Team" plan pricing in the JSON
# The pricing data is in a JSON structure with "internalName" fields
# Look for "Team" plan pricing
team_pricing = re.findall(r'"Team".{0,500}?"price".{0,50}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
print("Team pricing JSON:", team_pricing[:3])

# Look for "Enterprise" plan pricing
ent_pricing = re.findall(r'"Enterprise".{0,500}?"price".{0,50}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
print("Enterprise pricing JSON:", ent_pricing[:3])

# Look for all pricing entries with "internalName"
pricing_entries = re.findall(r'"internalName"[^"]*"[^"]*"[^"]*"\$?(\d+\.?\d*)', content)
print("Pricing entries:", pricing_entries[:20])

# Look for "Team" plan pricing in a different way
# Search for "Team" plan with "per user" or "per month"
team_per_user = re.findall(r'Team.{0,500}?(?:per\s+user|per\s+month|per\s+seat)', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
print("Team per user contexts:", len(team_per_user))
for t in team_per_user[:3]:
    t_clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', t)
    t_clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', t_clean).strip()
    print(f"  ...{t_clean[:300]}...")
PYEOF

# Let me try to get Bitwarden pricing from a different source
# Try to get pricing from a comparison site
curl -sL "https://www.softwareadvice.com/password-management/top-products/2024/" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "

content = sys.stdin.read()
print(f'Page size: {len(content)}')
prices = re.findall(r'\$\d+\.?\d{2}', content)
print(f'Prices found: {sorted(set(prices))[:30]}')
"
